notes:
Call of Legends (col1) is a transitional HGSS-era reprint set: 95 main-set cards + 11 Shiny Legendary "SL" secret cards = 106 total; HGSS-era booster = 10 cards/pack, 36 packs/box, NO ETB. This set has NO Primes or LEGENDs of its own — the only chase cards are the SL shinies, which sit in the holo tier and are mapped onto the rare-holo bucket.

PACK STRUCTURE (era baseline, confirmed 10 cards/pack by Pokemon.com & Bulbapedia): 5 common, 3 uncommon, 1 rare slot, 1 reverse-holo slot.

RARE SLOT: Resolves to a regular rare or a rare-holo. SL Shiny Legendaries pull at ~1:18 packs (Flipside Gaming era review), i.e. ~2 per 36-pack box — consistent with PokeBeach box reports of 1-4 shinies/box (avg ~2). Main-set rare-holos seed at roughly the standard era ~1:3 holo rate. Net per rare slot: rare-holo ~0.33 (SL ~0.055 folded in via the holo tier + main holos ~0.275), regular rare ~0.67. This matches box reports of ~1-2 main holos + ~2 SL = ~3-4 holos across 36 rare slots (~0.30 holo).

REVERSE-HOLO SLOT: In this era the reverse slot draws from common/uncommon/rare across the set. Distributed proportional to non-holo set counts (common 31, uncommon 25, rare 17; total 73): common 0.42, uncommon 0.35, rare 0.23.

CONFIDENCE low: no large per-set published pull-rate study exists for this vintage set. Figures combine Flipside Gaming era-average rates (SL ~1:18, Primes 1:6-7 / LEGENDs 1:12-18 — neither present in this set) with small community box samples from PokeBeach. Vintage sealed box carries a large collector premium, so calculated EV/pack will sit far below box price; that is expected and rates were NOT inflated to compensate.
